user_query:
I'm preparing to merge a small backend PR and want a very high-signal adversarial code review before it goes in. Please do the following:

1) Read the PR materials under input/:
- input/diff.txt (git diff for this PR)
- input/pr_description.md (intent and notes)
- input/design.md (minimal design context)

2) Apply a three-stage adversarial review process: reconstruct builder context (intent summary), run a brutally critical reviewer pass assuming subtle bugs/security issues are likely, then run a meta-review that filters to only high-confidence AND high-priority findings. The final deliverable should contain no more than 3 comments and can be fewer if appropriate.

3) Write the final filtered review to output/review.json using this exact JSON schema (an array of 1–3 objects):
[
  {
    "file": "<path from diff, e.g., src/api/users.py>",
    "line_range": "<start-end integers, e.g., 4-8>",
    "issue": "<one-sentence description>",
    "impact": "<why it matters>",
    "suggested_fix": "<concrete code-level fix>",
    "confidence": "high",
    "priority": "high"
  }
]
- Only include issues that are truly high confidence and high priority. Keep the issue field to a single sentence.
- The file value must match a path present in input/diff.txt. The line_range must be in the form "N-M" with positive integers.
- confidence and priority must both be exactly "high".

4) Also write a brief process trace to output/trace.md that includes:
- A short builder context summary (what the PR is trying to do in your own words)
- The critical reviewer’s raw findings as bullet points (include at least 3 bullets even if you ultimately filter down)
- The meta-reviewer’s rationale for which findings were kept vs. rejected and why

Make sure all outputs are written under output/ and that your final review strictly follows the schema and high-signal constraints.
